items.mistake: Using a mutable default directly: tags: list = [].
items.why: A bare [] is shared across all instances, the classic mutable-default bug.

items.mistake: Manually checking 'if key in d' before appending when grouping.
items.why: defaultdict creates the empty list on first access, simplifying the code.

items.mistake: Using list.pop(0) / insert(0, x) for a queue.
items.fix: Use collections.deque, which is O(1) at both ends.
items.why: list operations at the front are O(n) because elements shift.
items.example: Processing 10k items with `list.pop(0)` → O(n²) ≈ 100M ops. `deque.popleft()` → O(n) ≈ 10k ops. Same code, 10,000× faster.

items.mistake: Probing a defaultdict with d[k] just to check existence.
items.fix: Use 'k in d' or d.get(k); reserve d[k] for intended insertion.
items.why: Indexing a defaultdict inserts the key as a side effect.
items.example:
```python
d = defaultdict(list)
if d['missing']: ...   # OOPS — 'missing' is now in d, mapped to []
if 'missing' in d: ... # correct: pure check, no side effect
```
